_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Enlarging pelvic mass despite medical therapy (requiring laparotomy)
Primary unresponsiveness to medical treatment (for chronic PID)
Persistence or enlargement of adenexal mass after 4 - 6 months of treatment (for chronic PID)
Persistence or recurrence of pelvic pain on treatment (for chronic PID)

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings and advice for admission
Hemogram with Erythrocyte sedimentation rate, liver function test, renal function test, serum electrolytes, blood culture
USG abdomen and pelvis (if adnexal mass)

claim.label: Blood transfusion notes (if blood transfusion was given)

ppd_preauth.q: Was clinical presentation, severity, imaging and investigations indicative of surgery?
Was the requirement of admission documented (severe illness or nausea and vomiting/HIV positive, unable to follow or tolerate an outpatient regimen and outpatient therapy failed, others)?

common_queries: Clinical presentation, severity, imaging, and investigations not indicative of surgery for PID.
Requirement for inpatient admission not documented or justified based on severe illness, nausea, vomiting, HIV positive status, or outpatient therapy failure.
USG abdomen and pelvis (if adnexal mass present) not submitted or not confirming the diagnosis.
Mandatory laboratory investigations (Hemogram, ESR, LFT, RFT, serum electrolytes, blood culture) not submitted or incomplete.
Operative notes not detailing the surgical steps or findings for PID.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Facilities with well-equipped operation theatre, anesthesia and anesthetist availability
clinical_key_pointers: Pelvic Inflammatory disease (PID) is a spectrum of infections involving female upper genital tract i.e. cervix, uterus, tubes, ovaries and pelvic peritoneum. The disease may have acute or chronic presentation.
Proceed with the PID, after diagnosis is confirmed by the clinical manifestations, and backed by the reasons for such symptoms.
surgical_options: Colpotomy for drainage of midline pelvic abscess
Dilatation and evacuation of septic products of conception in post-abortal sepsis
Laparoscopy (if diagnosis is uncertain, no response to treatment, to reconfirm the diagnosis, obtain cultures from cul de sac and fallopian tubes and drain pus if necessary)
Total abdominal hysterectomy with bilateral salpingooophorectomy (for definitive surgery in chronic PID)
